title: Why is Rei so universally hated
display_title: Why is Rei so universally hated
wikitext:
[]: Why is Rei so universally hated? Post by Tlaloc » Tue Jul 21, 2009 10:19 pm Hello there! It seems none has made this thread, and as since Shinji and Asuka have already theirs, why not Rei? But this is not just because "to get in the mood and not leave Rei behind," it is because I actually notice that nowadays NGE fans are starting to dislike Rei. Now, I am new in this board; and I fear that because of it some may not take me seriously and see me as a "n00b" but I do want to make this thread. Let's see, I first knew of NGE because of the GameFan magazines. I bought one because in them they had stores that sold toy and other items from Japan, and I wanted some of Dragon Ball and Sailor Moon. While looking at the magazine, I noticed they had an anime review section, and in it they dedicated an entire page to the 7th volume of NGE. At first I had no idea what series it was, but the GF people kept saying it was the best anime ever and in a side to the review, there was this large image of an odd haunting girl. At first I didn't knew her name, but after some issues of the magazine; I found out she was Rei Ayanami. I wanted to see more of Rei, so in early 1998, I bought my first NGE VHS volume, which was vol 9. Honestly, for me if it weren't for Rei I'd probably would not had been interested in NGE. Her character seemed interesting for me, quiet, mysterious yet full of grace and class. Never I had seen someone quiet as interesting as her; sure, I saw other interesting anime girls, but Rei had her own kind of vibe. But now, I have noticed people just diss on her; even people who used to be fans of her. Many say it is because they have matured, and see Asuka more interesting because she has more personality than Rei, plus a soul. Some even want to break their friendship with me for not supporting Asuka, and consider me immature, childish and narrow minded. I think they are taking it too seriously, but really? Why do you think now people hate Rei now after these years since NGE became known in the western world?

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 21, 2009 11:09 pm Didn't Ritsko say that Rei was the only synthetic body to be able to contain a soul ? I don't think she is universally hated. She really doesn't do enough in the series for anybody to hate her. Even people I know who've hated Evangelion after watching it, still liked Rei. As for me. I can find a reason to sympathize with any female Evangelion character. It helps me to understand them better. And Rei's an interesting case. She's almost identical to Shinji, in that they are both trying to discover why they exist in their situation. As a matter of fact, the two even make new discoveries about themselves around the same time. But Shinji tries to run away from things he doesn't understand, whereas Rei just "rolls with the punches" about her mysterious existence. Interestingly enough, it was her relationship with Shinji that caused her to start to question herself and her loyalties. "Thank you"... Words of gratitude. Words I've never used. Not even with him.

[]: Post by schismatics » Tue Jul 21, 2009 11:41 pm I'm neutral about Rei. Neither hate or live.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Tue Jul 21, 2009 11:59 pm How can you say people hate Rei when they always say stuff like: ZOMG TEH Rei!!!!1

[]: Post by Soul Fire » Wed Jul 22, 2009 12:02 am I think hundreds of thousands dollars worth of sold Rei merchandise says other wise with regard to wether or not the majority of people hate her.

[]: Post by Rock-Slash » Wed Jul 22, 2009 12:18 am Rei isn't hated, even REIchu loves her, and she hate her, oxymoron? maybe. I myself don't like her very much, her bad traits overshadow her good ones, at least for me, but not even close enough to hate her.

[]: Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Jul 22, 2009 12:25 am So do you mean universally hated as in the Evangelion universe?

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2009 4:13 am This thread's title automatically dooms the conversation. The various "DO YOU LIEK TEH Rei?" discussions in Chit-Chat manage to prompt the reverse ("Here's why I don't like TEH Rei") enough that I'm just going to lock this.
